title: LocalRAG: Local RAG for your documents
home_seo_title: Local AI search for documents - LocalRAG
home_meta_description: Ask questions over PDFs, notes, tables, and code through local RAG on Ollama. Check answers against retrieved source fragments.
home_hero_title: Local AI search for private documents.
home_hero_subhead: Connect a folder with PDFs, scans, notes, tables, and code. Ask questions through local RAG on Ollama and check answers against retrieved source fragments.
home_hero_microcopy: First run requires Docker Compose and a local Ollama runtime.
proof_local_files: Files stay local in the default workflow
proof_visible_settings: Model and retrieval settings are visible in the UI
problem_heading: Real folders rarely look like demo sets.
problem_body: Work documents mix PDFs, scans, Markdown, tables, code, several languages, and imperfect file names. A normal chat can sound convincing without showing which fragment shaped the answer. LocalRAG is built around local search, visible settings, and source checks.
promise_index_body: Connect documents, refresh the index, and work with files without a required upload workflow.
promise_context_body: The question goes through retrieval, and the answer is paired with found fragments.
promise_sources_body: Open the file path, pages, and lines when those metadata are available.
promise_settings_body: Choose the model, answer language, role, and retrieval parameters directly in the interface.
privacy_body: In the default workflow, LocalRAG uses a folder on your computer, builds a local index, and uses Ollama models. The final privacy boundary depends on your configuration, network settings, selected models, and connected external services.
source_trust_heading: You do not have to take the answer on faith.
source_trust_body: LocalRAG shows retrieved fragments, file paths, pages, and lines where available. It does not guarantee a perfect answer, but it gives you a clear place to check.
workflow_step_add_body: Place files in the selected local folder.
workflow_step_reindex_body: LocalRAG builds a FAISS index with metadata.
workflow_step_ask_body: Choose an Ollama model, answer language, and role.
workflow_step_sources_body: Compare the answer with the retrieved fragments.
workflow_step_quality_body: Adjust top-k, model, or embedding when needed.
quality_heading: Quality checks are not hidden away.
quality_body: LocalRAG includes API tests, release smoke checks, and an eval set for answers over mixed corpora. This helps catch retrieval regressions instead of only checking that the server started.
final_cta_heading: Try LocalRAG on a small folder.
final_cta_body: Start with documents whose answers you can verify manually. That makes it easier to judge model quality, retrieval, and source usefulness on your own corpus.
footer_short_copy: LocalRAG is local RAG for document questions with checkable sources, Ollama model control, and a multilingual interface.
file_info: Put PDF, DOCX, TXT, MD, HTML, PY, JS, JSON, CSV, YAML files in the documents folder and press reindex!
status_no_documents: No documents found. Add files to the folder.
status_saved_failed: Index built but could not be saved to disk. Future runs will reindex from scratch.
context_placeholder: Context will appear here after you ask a question.
invalid_topk: Invalid top_k value. Please choose between 1 and 50.
empty_question: Please enter a question before submitting.
models_unavailable: Models unavailable. Check the Ollama service.
role_analyst_desc: Structured analysis with risks and evidence.
role_engineer_desc: Implementation-first answer with steps and checks.
settings_docs_path_hint: Enter the full path manually or use Choose folder to open the folder picker. LocalRAG will work directly with files in the selected folder.
docs_browser_search_placeholder: Filter folders by name or path
docs_browser_error: Failed to load the folder list.
settings_role_prompts_hint: These prompts are saved in your browser per answer language and applied to the selected role.
settings_hint: These settings are saved in your browser and applied to new answers.
response_stale: Inputs changed. Submit the question again.
settings_role_images_hint: Each role card can use its own illustration preset. The selection is saved in your browser.
settings_custom_roles_hint: Create shared roles with their own illustration, description, answer language, default model, default style, and master prompt. They appear next to the built-in roles for every browser connected to this instance.
settings_custom_roles_description_placeh: Short note shown on the role card.
settings_custom_roles_prompt_placeholder: Describe how this role should answer and format responses.
settings_custom_roles_prompt_required: Enter a master prompt for the role.
settings_model_manager_hint: See installed models, download new ones, remove old ones, and pick a default model for this browser.
models_manual_hint: This field accepts Ollama model tags only. If a model is not available in the Ollama library, the download will fail. Hugging Face repositories belong in the embedding model section above.
models_invalid_name: Enter a valid model name, for example qwen3:8b.
models_pull_busy: Another model download is already running: {model}.
models_not_found_in_ollama: Model {model} was not found in the Ollama library.
model_catalog_qwen3_5_9b: Current best multilingual default under 10B with stronger answer grounding.
model_catalog_qwen3_14b: Higher quality multilingual option with a larger footprint.
model_catalog_gemma3_12b: Strong multilingual alternative from the Gemma 3 family.
model_catalog_aya_expanse_8b: Multilingual specialist. Check the non-commercial license before production use.
settings_embedding_model_hint: Choose the retrieval embedding model used for indexing. Changing it clears the current index and starts a full reindex. New models may be downloaded automatically on first use.
settings_embedding_model_custom_placehol: Enter a Hugging Face model id or a local path
settings_embedding_model_invalid: Enter an embedding model or local path.
settings_embedding_model_selected: Selected embedding model: {model}. Click Apply settings to reindex.
settings_embedding_model_manager_hint: Choose the embedding model used for indexing, prepare recommended models, or verify a custom Hugging Face model id before switching.
embedding_models_prepare_busy: Another embedding model is already being prepared: {model}.
embedding_models_invalid_name: Enter a Hugging Face model id or a local path.
embedding_model_catalog_e5_large: Stable multilingual baseline with strong retrieval quality.
embedding_model_catalog_bge_m3: Newer multilingual retrieval model with broader language coverage and longer context support.
embedding_model_catalog_gte_multilingual: Modern multilingual embedding alternative worth testing against the current baseline.
hero_visual_alt: LocalRAG interface beside a local document folder, a retrieval graph, and a retrieved source card.
walkthrough_heading: 75 seconds from folder to checkable answer.
walkthrough_body: The walkthrough poster and script show the baseline path: choose a folder, reindex files, ask a question, open sources, and check model settings. The full clip remains a separate release asset so heavy video is not committed before storage is approved.
walkthrough_poster_alt: LocalRAG walkthrough poster with a local folder, retrieval graph, source card, and answer interface.
walkthrough_caption: Canonical clip flow: local folder, FAISS, Ollama, sources, and pre-release checks.
